authoritative_sources.title: The NIST Definition of Cloud Computing
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines cloud service and actor model assumptions so the lane can separate tenant-owned access/authority context from CSP-provided infrastructure control.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: service model framing, access ownership, and boundary separation
used_for: deciding where tenant policy and specialist role boundaries start and end
grounding terminology for cloud-facing operations handoffs
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Used when normalizing incident requests involving cloud tenants, shared responsibility assumptions, and access-sensitive outputs.

authoritative_sources.title: Cloud Computing Synopsis and Recommendations
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides operating guidance on cloud lifecycle considerations and dependency assumptions that directly inform execution readiness and integration risk.
used_for: setting workflow assumptions for inter-system integration and operational sequencing
identifying where assumptions must be tenant-confirmed
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Used in scenario-level checks for interface/dependency failures and cross-system handoff logic.

authoritative_sources.title: Computer Security Incident Handling Guide
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Canonical incident response reference that defines evidence, escalation, and closure expectations used for safe execution decisions.
used_for: defining minimum evidence required before completion claims
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Used for boundary-safe refusal and exception handling when security or containment evidence is not complete.

authoritative_sources.title: Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines control expectations and evidence artifacts for access, logging, continuity, and incident tracking used in completion checks.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: evidence traceability, access validation, and audit readiness
used_for: minimum control evidence expectations in completion records
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Used to constrain completion outputs to auditable records and avoid unauthorized closure claims.

authoritative_sources.title: Contingency Planning Guide for Federal Information Systems
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides continuity planning principles that inform rollback, failover, and recovery-oriented completion checks.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Used in escalation scenarios requiring testing/rollback evidence or recovery confirmation before completion.

authoritative_sources.title: Uptime Tier Standard
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Industry-recognized resilience framework that influences how data center operators describe outage severity, redundancy state, and recovery posture.
used_for: avoidance of generic generic sequencing assumptions
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Used for scenario 6 and completion/priority messaging where language must be explicitly infrastructure-aware.

workflow_stages.name: Acceptance and context capture
workflow_stages.description: Ingest accepted operational requests, lock request scope, and confirm minimal authoritative context from tenant systems before progression.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Execution readiness and dependency validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate incident, access, change, and inter-system dependency evidence before advancing state.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception handling and escalation
workflow_stages.description: Handle missing artifacts, policy conflicts, failed dependencies, and authority mismatches through explicit queue and handoff structures.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence capture
workflow_stages.description: Collect completion artifacts, rollback proof, approver context, and closeout rationale with explicit traceability.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Tenant-specific recovery and adaptation
workflow_stages.description: Incorporate tenant-specific critical-load lists, authority matrices, and contractual escalation requirements before final closure.

artifacts: Incident tickets and impact status records
Identity and access management proofs (MFA, role change logs, session revocations)
Change tickets, CAB approvals, test and rollback evidence
Dependency maps for network, power, cooling, and external interfaces
Runbooks, failure playbooks, and restoration summaries
Maintenance windows and critical workload priority matrices
Exception queue records and escalation handoff notes
systems_of_record: Incident management system (e.g., ServiceNow, Jira Service Management)
IT service management and request catalog systems
Change management and release control systems
Cloud operations telemetry and deployment systems
Network/power provider event channels and escalation queues

allowed: Assess execution readiness for incidents, access verification, change-readiness, and interface dependency controls
Escalate dependency or policy blockers to tenant or adjacent specialist owners
Issue completion evidence maps tied to verified artifacts
escalate: Conflicting records across systems require reconciliation by owner
Security, compliance, financial-approval, or legal/policy decisions are required
Power/network/provider failure materially changes execution path
Tenant policy defines stricter criticality tiers than public baselines
refuse: Requests for authority not delegated to this lane
Requests to bypass rollback and test obligations

domain_failure_modes: Concluding completion from generic support assumptions without explicit data-center dependency and access evidence
Proceeding on stale access records where privileged roles remain unresolved
Masking unresolved cross-team dependency failures by claiming local completion
Accepting change requests that lack rollback proof and approval confirmation
Overgeneralizing cloud operations language without tenant-specific criticality and SLO context

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Execution exception requires rework or escalation
Tenant-specific adaptation that preserves public baseline

unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Sovereign cloud, financial-services controls, or national cyber rules may alter access and retention obligations
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant policy and authority matrix at runtime and route unresolved cases to tenant owner instead of inferring obligations from public material.

unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Some tenants are colocation, edge, or hybrid-facility operators with different outage and dependency semantics
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant topology map and facility taxonomy before advanced prioritization and completion recommendations.

unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Approver and rollback authority can be split across CSP, tenant platform owner, and enterprise change boards
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Keep completion refusal and escalation route explicit until tenant authority owner confirms the exact approval chain.

notes: This lane is materially specific to data-center and cloud execution work because access role context, dependency validation, and interface failure handling materially alter behavior from generic operations support.

The authoritative source set is intentionally balanced across workflow, evidence-control, and resilience guidance, with all policy and regulatory edge cases delegated to tenant-provided policy sources.
No blocking unresolved ambiguities remain; all ambiguous points are converted into explicit required-runtime checks or escalation conditions.
